---
title: "生存分析模仿案例"
author: "黄国政"
date: "2023-10-08"
categories: [机器学习]
image: "image.jpg"
toc: true
---


::: {.callout-note apperance="simple"}
## Pay attention

本生存分析模仿案例根据教程[Survival on the RMS Titanic](https://modeloriented.github.io/DALEX/articles/vignette_titanic.html)翻译而来，我在翻译中增加了大量来自个人理解的解读。
:::

## 泰坦尼克号的生存数据

让我们以泰坦尼克号数据集分析问题的分类模型中的`DALEX`包为例，引用的数据集是来自其中的`titanic`。这份数据是从`stablelearner`包复制而来。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(DALEX)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Welcome to DALEX (version: 2.4.3).
Find examples and detailed introduction at: http://ema.drwhy.ai/
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
head(titanic_imputed)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
  gender age class    embarked  fare sibsp parch survived
1   male  42   3rd Southampton  7.11     0     0        0
2   male  13   3rd Southampton 20.05     0     2        0
3   male  16   3rd Southampton 20.05     1     1        0
4 female  39   3rd Southampton 20.05     1     1        1
5 female  16   3rd Southampton  7.13     0     0        1
6   male  25   3rd Southampton  7.13     0     0        1
```
:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
?titanic_imputed
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
starting httpd help server ... done
```
:::
:::


运行以上代码，Rstudio的帮助界面中会出现`titanic_imputed`的详细信息，我们可以据此了解该数据集中各变量的含义。

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## 各变量含义

1.  `gender`：性别
2.  `age`：年龄
3.  `class`：乘客舱位等级或船员服务类型
4.  `embarked`：登船地点
5.  `fare`：票价（船员、音乐家和船厂员工的为0）
6.  `sibsp`：同胞或配偶的数量
7.  `parch`：父母或子女的数量
8.  `survival`：是否生存
:::

## 泰坦尼克号的生存模型

使用随机森林模型试试。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ranger)
model_titanic_rf <- ranger(survived ~ gender + age + class + embarked +fare + sibsp + parch,  data = titanic_imputed, classification = TRUE)
model_titanic_rf
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Ranger result

Call:
 ranger(survived ~ gender + age + class + embarked + fare + sibsp +      parch, data = titanic_imputed, classification = TRUE) 

Type:                             Classification 
Number of trees:                  500 
Sample size:                      2207 
Number of independent variables:  7 
Mtry:                             2 
Target node size:                 1 
Variable importance mode:         none 
Splitrule:                        gini 
OOB prediction error:             19.57 % 
```
:::
:::


`model_titanic_rf`即我们创建的一个随机森林模型（random forest model）

## 对泰坦尼克号生存分析的解释

第三步是为随机森林模型创建一个`DALEX`解释。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(DALEX)
explain_titanic_rf <- explain(model_titanic_rf, 
                      data = titanic_imputed,
                      y = titanic_imputed$survived, 
                      label = "Random Forest v7",
                      colorize = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Preparation of a new explainer is initiated
  -> model label       :  Random Forest v7 
  -> data              :  2207  rows  8  cols 
  -> target variable   :  2207  values 
  -> predict function  :  yhat.ranger  will be used (  default  )
  -> predicted values  :  No value for predict function target column. (  default  )
  -> model_info        :  package ranger , ver. 0.15.1 , task classification (  default  ) 
  -> predicted values  :  numerical, min =  0 , mean =  0.2106932 , max =  1  
  -> residual function :  difference between y and yhat (  default  )
  -> residuals         :  numerical, min =  -1 , mean =  0.1105573 , max =  1  
  A new explainer has been created!  
```
:::
:::


## 变量重要性的可视化

使用`variable_importance()`来说明特定特征的重要性。`type="difference"`会使dropout一般化，现在它们从0开始。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vi_rf <- model_parts(explain_titanic_rf)
head(vi_rf)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
      variable mean_dropout_loss            label
1 _full_model_         0.2181517 Random Forest v7
2     survived         0.2183520 Random Forest v7
3        parch         0.2321642 Random Forest v7
4        sibsp         0.2363582 Random Forest v7
5     embarked         0.2398044 Random Forest v7
6         fare         0.2638749 Random Forest v7
```
:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(vi_rf)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


问题来了，这张图应该如何进行解释呢......

## 变量效应

由上图，我们知道性别是最突出的特征，其他三个比较突出的特征分别是舱位等级、年龄和票价。试着探究模型响应变量和这些特征之间分别的联系。

这种单变量关系可以用`variable_effect()`来计算。

### age年龄

5岁及以下的儿童有着更高的存活率。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vr_age <- model_profile(explain_titanic_rf, variables =  "age")
head(vr_age)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
$cp_profiles

485.5   male 13.0000000   3rd Southampton 7.1506     0     0        0      0

$color

```
:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(vr_age)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### class舱位等级

头等舱的乘客有着更高的存活率。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vr_class <- model_profile(explain_titanic_rf, variables =  "class")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
'variable_type' changed to 'categorical' due to lack of numerical variables.
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(vr_class)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### fare票价

廉价的船票与更低的机会相关


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vr_fare <- variable_profile(explain_titanic_rf, variables =  "fare")
plot(vr_fare)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### embarked

从C处登船的乘客存活率最高


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vr_embarked <- model_profile(explain_titanic_rf, variables =  "embarked")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
'variable_type' changed to 'categorical' due to lack of numerical variables.
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(vr_embarked)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## 实例层面的解释

看看从C处登船的头等舱的8岁男性的模型预测细分解释。


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
new_passanger <- data.frame(
  class = factor("1st", levels = c("1st", "2nd", "3rd", "deck crew", "engineering crew", "restaurant staff", "victualling crew")),
  gender = factor("male", levels = c("female", "male")),
  age = 8,
  sibsp = 0,
  parch = 0,
  fare = 72,
  embarked = factor("Southampton", levels = c("Belfast", "Cherbourg", "Queenstown", "Southampton"))
)

sp_rf <- predict_parts(explain_titanic_rf, new_passanger)
plot(sp_rf)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


由上图可以看出，这位乘客最重要的特征是年龄和性别。毕竟他的生存几率比一般乘客要高，主要是因为年龄小，虽然是男性。

## 更多模型

试一试使用更多的模型来进行生存分析

### Logistic回归分析


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library("rms")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: Hmisc
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```

Attaching package: 'Hmisc'
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    format.pval, units
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
model_titanic_lmr <- lrm(survived ~ class + gender + rcs(age) + sibsp +
                   parch + fare + embarked, titanic_imputed)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
number of knots in rcs defaulting to 5
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
explain_titanic_lmr <- explain(model_titanic_lmr, data = titanic_imputed, 
                   y = titanic_imputed$survived, 
                   predict_function = function(m,x) 
                            predict(m, x, type = "fitted"),
                   label = "Logistic regression")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Preparation of a new explainer is initiated
  -> model label       :  Logistic regression 
  -> data              :  2207  rows  8  cols 
  -> target variable   :  2207  values 
  -> predict function  :  function(m, x) predict(m, x, type = "fitted") 
  -> predicted values  :  No value for predict function target column. (  default  )

  -> predicted values  :  numerical, min =  0.002671631 , mean =  0.3221568 , max =  0.9845724  
  -> residual function :  difference between y and yhat (  default  )
  -> residuals         :  numerical, min =  -0.9845724 , mean =  -2.491758e-09 , max =  0.9715125  
  A new explainer has been created!  
```
:::
:::


### Generalized Boosted 模型


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library("gbm")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loaded gbm 2.1.8.1
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
model_titanic_gbm <- gbm(survived  ~ class + gender + age + sibsp +
                     parch + fare + embarked, data = titanic_imputed, 
                     n.trees = 15000)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Distribution not specified, assuming bernoulli ...
```
:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
explain_titanic_gbm <- explain(model_titanic_gbm, data = titanic_imputed, 
                       y = titanic_imputed$survived, 
                       predict_function = function(m,x) 
                            predict(m, x, n.trees = 15000, type = "response"),
                       label = "Generalized Boosted Models",
                       colorize = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Preparation of a new explainer is initiated
  -> model label       :  Generalized Boosted Models 
  -> data              :  2207  rows  8  cols 
  -> target variable   :  2207  values 
  -> predict function  :  function(m, x) predict(m, x, n.trees = 15000, type = "response") 
  -> predicted values  :  No value for predict function target column. (  default  )
  -> model_info        :  package gbm , ver. 2.1.8.1 , task classification (  default  ) 
  -> predicted values  :  numerical, min =  0.0002164674 , mean =  0.3226692 , max =  0.9987116  
  -> residual function :  difference between y and yhat (  default  )
  -> residuals         :  numerical, min =  -0.9683219 , mean =  -0.0005124644 , max =  0.9920892  
  A new explainer has been created!  
```
:::
:::


### Support Vector Machines (SVM)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library("e1071")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```

Attaching package: 'e1071'
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
The following object is masked from 'package:Hmisc':

    impute
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
model_titanic_svm <- svm(survived ~ class + gender + age + sibsp + parch + fare + embarked, data = titanic_imputed, 
             type = "C-classification", probability = TRUE)
explain_titanic_svm <- explain(model_titanic_svm, data = titanic_imputed, 
                       y = titanic_imputed$survived, 
                       label = "Support Vector Machines",
                       colorize = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Preparation of a new explainer is initiated
  -> model label       :  Support Vector Machines 
  -> data              :  2207  rows  8  cols 
  -> target variable   :  2207  values 
  -> predict function  :  yhat.svm  will be used (  default  )
  -> predicted values  :  No value for predict function target column. (  default  )
  -> model_info        :  package e1071 , ver. 1.7.13 , task classification (  default  ) 
  -> predicted values  :  numerical, min =  0.08720145 , mean =  0.3225264 , max =  0.9622115  
  -> residual function :  difference between y and yhat (  default  )
  -> residuals         :  numerical, min =  -0.8647758 , mean =  -0.0003696164 , max =  0.9127986  
  A new explainer has been created!  
```
:::
:::


### k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library("caret")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: ggplot2
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: lattice
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
model_titanic_knn <- knn3(survived ~ class + gender + age + sibsp + parch + fare + embarked, data = titanic_imputed, k = 5)
explain_titanic_knn <- explain(model_titanic_knn, data = titanic_imputed, 
                       y = titanic_imputed$survived, 
                       predict_function = function(m,x) predict(m, x)[,2],
                       label = "k-Nearest Neighbors",
                       colorize = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Preparation of a new explainer is initiated
  -> model label       :  k-Nearest Neighbors 
  -> data              :  2207  rows  8  cols 
  -> target variable   :  2207  values 
  -> predict function  :  function(m, x) predict(m, x)[, 2] 
  -> predicted values  :  No value for predict function target column. (  default  )
  -> model_info        :  package Model of class: knn3 package unrecognized , ver. Unknown , task regression (  default  ) 
  -> predicted values  :  numerical, min =  0 , mean =  0.3061413 , max =  1  
  -> residual function :  difference between y and yhat (  default  )
  -> residuals         :  numerical, min =  -0.8333333 , mean =  0.01601551 , max =  0.9285714  
  A new explainer has been created!  
```
:::
:::


### Variable performance


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vi_rf <- model_parts(explain_titanic_rf)
vi_lmr <- model_parts(explain_titanic_lmr)
vi_gbm <- model_parts(explain_titanic_gbm)
vi_svm <- model_parts(explain_titanic_svm)
vi_knn <- model_parts(explain_titanic_knn)

plot(vi_rf, vi_lmr, vi_gbm, vi_svm, vi_knn, bar_width = 4)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-18-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### Single variable


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vr_age_rf   <- model_profile(explain_titanic_rf, variables = "age")
vr_age_lmr  <- model_profile(explain_titanic_lmr, variables = "age")
vr_age_gbm  <- model_profile(explain_titanic_gbm, variables = "age")
vr_age_svm  <- model_profile(explain_titanic_svm, variables = "age")
vr_age_knn  <- model_profile(explain_titanic_knn, variables = "age")
plot(vr_age_rf$agr_profiles, 
     vr_age_lmr$agr_profiles, 
     vr_age_gbm$agr_profiles, 
     vr_age_svm$agr_profiles, 
     vr_age_knn$agr_profiles)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-19-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## 事例层面阐释
